name: Pre-mortem
description: Assume the plan shipped and failed catastrophically 6 months from now. Work backwards to the causes.

name: Inversion
description: Instead of asking how to succeed, enumerate everything that would guarantee failure, then check the target against that list.
core_question: How would we guarantee this fails?

name: First Principles
description: Strip every assumption inherited by analogy or habit; rebuild the reasoning from verifiable base facts only.
core_question: What do we actually know to be true here?
best_for: Inherited designs, 'we've always done it this way'

name: Red Team vs Blue Team
description: Split into an attacker seeking to break the proposal and a defender patching each attack; alternate until attacks repeat.
core_question: How would an adversary exploit this?
best_for: Security decisions, APIs, trust boundaries, incentives

name: Steelman-then-attack
description: Build the strongest possible version of the opposing position before criticizing the target; only then compare.
core_question: What is the best case AGAINST our choice?

name: Chesterton's Fence
description: For every element the proposal removes or replaces, explain why it existed before touching it.
core_question: Why was the old thing there?

description: Evaluate the decision's consequences at 10 minutes, 10 months and 10 years.
core_question: How does this look in 10 minutes / 10 months / 10 years?

name: Second-Order Effects
description: Ignore the direct effect; enumerate what the effect causes next — who adapts, what breaks, what incentives shift.

name: Occam's Razor
description: Find the simplest explanation or design that accounts for all the hard requirements; flag every element the simple version lacks.
core_question: What is the simplest thing that could work?

name: Constraint Removal
description: Pick the binding constraint (budget, stack, deadline, team) and redesign as if it vanished; harvest what transfers back.
core_question: What would we do without constraint X?

name: Persona Shift
description: Re-read the target as a novice, then as a domain expert, then as a hostile user; log what each one trips on.
core_question: What does a novice / expert / adversary see?
best_for: Docs, UX flows, onboarding, error messages

name: Cost of Delay
description: Quantify what waiting costs per week versus what shipping imperfect costs; make the time dimension explicit.
core_question: What does one more week cost us?
